title: Staff Firmware Design Engineer
description:
**Senior Firmware Design Engineer**
**We Are:**
 At Synopsys, we drive the innovations that shape the way we live and connect. Our technology is central to the Era of Pervasive Intelligence, from self\\-driving cars to learning machines. We lead in chip design, verification, and IP integration, empowering the creation of high\\-performance silicon chips and software content. Join us to transform the future through continuous technological innovation.
   

  

 At Synopsys, we’re at the heart of the innovations that change the way we work and play. Self\\-driving cars. Artificial Intelligence. The cloud. 5G. The Internet of Things. These breakthroughs are ushering in the Era of Smart Everything. And we’re powering it all with the world’s most advanced technologies for chip design and software security. If you share our passion for innovation, we want to meet you.
   

  

 Our Silicon IP business is all about integrating more capabilities into an SoC—faster. We offer the world’s broadest portfolio of silicon IP—predesigned blocks, memory, interfaces, analog, security, and embedded processors. All to help customers integrate more capabilities, meet unique performance, power, and size requirements of their target applications, and get differentiated products to market quickly with reduced risk.
   

  

**You Are:**
 You are a passionate and inventive engineer who thrives at the intersection of hardware and software. As a Firmware Staff Engineer, you bring a strong digital design background and a keen understanding of embedded software development, particularly in bare metal environments. You are motivated by the challenge of working at the cutting edge of mixed\\-signal and high\\-speed SerDes technology, continuously learning and applying new concepts to real\\-world solutions.
   

  

 You value collaboration and clear communication, adept at working across multidisciplinary teams to drive complex projects from concept to silicon validation. Your attention to detail ensures robust, reliable firmware, while your proactive mindset helps you anticipate issues and deliver high\\-quality results under tight deadlines. You are committed to structured development practices, embracing documentation and verification as essential parts of the engineering process.
   

  

 Your curiosity leads you to explore emerging standards and scripting tools, and you are always eager to share knowledge and mentor less experienced colleagues. Above all, you are driven by a desire to have a tangible impact on the technology that powers tomorrow’s innovations.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing:**
* Design, develop, and debug bare metal firmware for advanced mixed\\-signal and high\\-speed SerDes products.
* Collaborate with digital and mixed\\-signal design teams to define firmware requirements and system integration strategies.
* Lead and participate in the structured development, verification, and documentation of firmware solutions.
* Execute and drive projects from specification through to silicon bring\\-up and functional validation on prototype test\\-chips.
* Support internal and external customers by troubleshooting and resolving firmware\\-related issues in a timely manner.
* Continuously evaluate and adopt new methodologies, tools, and standards to improve firmware quality and development efficiency.
* Mentor junior engineers and contribute to a culture of knowledge sharing and technical excellence.


**The Impact You Will Have:**
* Accelerate the integration of advanced capabilities into customer SoCs, reducing product time\\-to\\-market.
* Enhance the reliability and performance of next\\-generation SerDes and mixed\\-signal products through robust firmware development.
* Enable seamless collaboration across design, verification, and application teams, ensuring delivery of high\\-quality solutions.
* Drive innovation by introducing new firmware techniques and best practices to the team.
* Support customer success by providing expert guidance and rapid resolution of firmware challenges.
* Contribute to Synopsys’ reputation as a leader in silicon IP and embedded systems solutions.


**What You’ll Need:**
* BSEE or MSEE with a minimum of 5 years of experience in bare metal firmware development and silicon testing.
* Strong understanding of digital design principles, including RTL\\-level concepts and methodologies.
* Proven experience with structured firmware development, verification, and comprehensive documentation processes.
* Demonstrated ability to drive projects from inception to successful completion, including silicon bring\\-up and validation.
* Excellent communication skills, enabling effective interaction with cross\\-functional teams and customers.
* Basic knowledge of SerDes standards and protocols is a plus.
* Experience with scripting languages such as Shell or Python is advantageous.


**Who You Are:**
* Self\\-motivated, proactive, and results\\-driven, with a passion for quality and innovation.
* Organized and detail\\-oriented, capable of managing multiple priorities and meeting tight deadlines.
* Collaborative team player who values clear and open communication.
* Adaptable and eager to learn new technologies and methodologies.
* Resourceful problem solver with a customer\\-centric mindset.


**The Team You’ll Be A Part Of:**
 You will join a highly experienced and diverse mixed\\-signal design and verification team dedicated to developing leading\\-edge SerDes and mixed\\-signal IP solutions. The team thrives on collaboration and continuous learning, working closely with digital, analog, and firmware experts to deliver innovative products from specification to silicon validation. You’ll have the opportunity to contribute to high\\-impact projects and grow your expertise alongside industry leaders.
   

  

**Rewards and Benefits:**
 We offer a comprehensive range of health, wellness, and financial benefits to cater to your needs. Our total rewards include both monetary and non\\-monetary offerings. Your recruiter will provide more details about the salary range and benefits during the hiring process.

description:

The role includes L3 production support, complex defect analysis and code fixes, proactive vulnerability remediation, and cybersecurity risk management.
* The candidate will act as the final escalation point for critical incidents, ensuring high availability of Banking application systems while maintaining strict compliance with banking regulatory and security standards. This role demands strong technical expertise, ownership mindset, and the ability to operate effectively in high\\-pressure financial environments.


**Key Responsibilities**
**Strategy**

**Processes**

 As a Principal Cloud Security Enablement Engineer, you will be responsible for leading the design, development, and strategic implementation of secure, scalable patterns and automation that empower development teams to embed security seamlessly into their software delivery workflows. In this role, you will architect and drive the integration and operationalization of tools such as Contrast Security, SonarQube, JFrog Xray, and CloudGuard (or equivalent) across CI/CD pipelines and cloud environments at scale. You will collaborate closely with engineering, security, and leadership teams to establish enterprise\\-wide security best practices, mentor senior engineers, and champion a culture of secure software development, enabling accelerated delivery without compromising security.
   

  

 Your entrepreneurial mindset and technical skills will be used, creating solutions that meet business needs and optimize customer experience directly impacting the organization and affecting the lives of millions.
   

  

 We believe that when people from diverse cultures, genders, and points of view come together, innovation is the result —and everyone wins. Medtronic walks the walk, creating an inclusive culture where you can thrive.
   

  

**Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned**
 In general, the following responsibilities apply for the
 **Principal Cloud Security Engineer** 
 role. This includes, but is not limited to the following:
   

  

* Security Tool Integration \\& Enablement
+ Drive the strategic selection, integration, and lifecycle management of application, artifact, and infrastructure security tools (e.g., Contrast, SonarQube, JFrog Xray, CloudGuard, Wi, etc) within enterprise CI/CD pipelines and cloud platforms.
+ Establish organization\\-wide standards, reusable automation frameworks, and “golden patterns” to ensure scalable, consistent, and secure adoption of security tools across diverse engineering teams.
+ Lead cross\\-functional initiatives with developers, cloud engineers, and DevOps teams to optimize security tooling usability and alignment with evolving workflows and business objectives.

* Automation \\& Process Development
+ Design and automate onboarding, configuration, and reporting for application and infrastructure security tools.
+ Contribute to standardized workflows and champion best practices for vulnerability management (detection, triage, and remediation) at both application and infrastructure levels.
+ Integrate and optimize security findings into enterprise developer and operational workflows (issue trackers, dashboards, alerting systems), ensuring actionable insights and measurable risk reduction.

* Cloud \\& Infrastructure Security Engineering
+ Set technical direction and provide guidance on cloud and infrastructure security engineering practices to safeguard code, dependencies, artifacts, and deployed workloads at scale.
+ Lead and influence enterprise\\-wide container, supply chain, and infrastructure security initiatives by designing and enforcing robust guardrails, automation, and governance models.
+ Leverage infrastructure security platforms (e.g., CloudGuard, Wiz, or equivalents) to provide actionable visibility and enforce cloud security policies.


* Collaboration \\& Knowledge Sharing
+ Serve as a key security thought leader and trusted advisor to engineering leadership, hands\\-on enabler for engineering teams, shaping the security enablement vision, roadmap, and organizational priorities.
+ Foster strong partnerships with product security, DevEx, operations, and compliance teams to align enablement with compliance and risk goals.
+ Promote a culture of security through knowledge sharing, technical leadership, and mentoring of engineers at all levels; drive internal security communities of practice and represent the organization in external forums as appropriate.


**Required Knowledge And Experience**
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related technical field.
* 10\\+ years of experience in cloud engineering, DevOps, or security engineering roles, with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and delivery of large\\-scale cloud security initiatives.
* Proven leadership in designing, architecting, and implementing secure cloud solutions across complex, multi\\-cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP, etc.).
* Extensive hands\\-on experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitHub, Azure DevOps, etc.), including designing and establishing enterprise\\-wide CI/CD best practices and frameworks.
* Deep working knowledge of application and artifact security tools (SonarQube, JFrog Xray, Contrast Security, etc.), including evaluating, selecting, and integrating new security technologies at scale.
* Experience leading adoption and integration of infrastructure security platforms (CloudGuard, Wiz, Orca, or equivalent), as well as logging and observability tooling (ELK, Dynatrace, etc.) across multiple teams or business units.
* Demonstrated experience in automating onboarding, configuration, and reporting for application and infrastructure security tools at an enterprise scale.
* Advanced proficiency in automation and scripting (Python, Go, or equivalent), including setting coding standards and mentoring others.
* Expert\\-level understanding of cloud environments (AWS), including security best practices, risk management, and compliance.


**Physical Job Requirements**
 The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this position, but they are not an exhaustive list of all the required responsibilities and skills of this position.
   

  

**Benefits \\& Compensation**
**Medtronic offers a competitive Salary and flexible Benefits Package**
 A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values. We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help to create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every career and life stage.
   

  

 This position is eligible for a short\\-term incentive called the Medtronic Incentive Plan (MIP).
   

  

**About Medtronic**
 We lead global healthcare technology and boldly attack the most challenging health problems facing humanity by searching out and finding solutions.
   

  

 Our Mission — to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life — unites a global team of 95,000\\+ passionate people.
   

  

 We are engineers at heart— putting ambitious ideas to work to generate real solutions for real people. From the R\\&D lab, to the factory floor, to the conference room, every one of us experiments, creates, builds, improves and solves. We have the talent, diverse perspectives, and guts to engineer the extraordinary.
   

  

 Learn more about our business, mission, and our commitment to diversity here

title: SME - Performance Engineering ( Load Runner Cloud)
description:
**WHO YOU ARE** 




  





 A highly skilled Subject Matter Expert (SME) in Performance Engineering to lead our efforts in ensuring system scalability, stability, and reliability across enterprise\\-grade applications. This is a strategic, hands\\-on role where you will apply deep technical expertise to deliver high\\-performing solutions that meet business\\-critical demands.
 



  





**WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING DAY TO DAY** 




  





 • Lead performance engineering initiatives across multiple systems—covering application and infrastructure layers—to ensure our SAP platforms perform optimally under forecasted peak loads.
   

 • Collaborate with technology architects, business stakeholders, and application providers to identify, assess, and mitigate performance risks proactively.
   

 • Design, plan, and execute performance testing strategies, including developing test scripts, configuring on\\-premises and cloud\\-based load generators, and analyzing performance results.
   

 • Drive root\\-cause analysis for performance bottlenecks, propose preventive actions, and ensure long\\-term system resilience.
   

 • Act as a bridge between internal and external stakeholders through clear communication, effective documentation, and precise performance reporting.
   

 • Define performance scope and align expectations across delivery teams, ensuring results are clearly communicated and risks are managed.
   

 • Contribute to strategic roadmap development by analyzing release strategies, identifying improvement opportunities, and introducing innovative approaches to performance engineering.
   

 • Work closely with cross\\-functional teams—including development, product management, and process teams—to ensure performance best practices are embedded throughout the lifecycle.
   

 • Mentor junior engineers, share best practices, and promote a culture of continuous improvement within the quality engineering domain.
   

  

 Key Qualifications we are looking for
   

  

 • 8\\-12 years of experience working across large and complex projects in Performance Test Engineering preferable with SAP products.
   

 • Good Hand on experience on performance testing lifecycle for SAP and integrated applications – from requirement gathering, planning, and scripting to execution and analysis.
   

 • Able to Design and develop performance test strategies and test plans aligned with business and technical objectives.
   

 • Hands on experience in Developing and maintaining integrated performance test scripts for SAP modules and supporting systems using LoadRunner Cloud and open\\-source tools.
   

 • Set up and configure test environments (on\\-prem or cloud) including load generators, monitoring tools, and data preparation.
   

 • Execute load, stress, endurance, and scalability tests and analyze results to identify bottlenecks and performance issues.
   

 • Collaborate closely with SAP Basis, infrastructure, and application teams to fine\\-tune systems and resolve performance bottlenecks.
   

 • Provide performance tuning recommendations across application code, database, and infrastructure layers.
   

 • Prepare and present detailed performance reports with actionable insights to technical and business stakeholders.
   

 • Contribute to performance engineering best practices, automation frameworks, and continuous improvement initiatives.
   

 • Stay updated on emerging tools, methodologies, and performance optimization techniques, especially within SAP and cloud environments.
   

  

 What We’re Looking For
   

 A Performance test expert who combines technical depth with strategic foresight, comes with Hands on Experience across different tools available in Market Preferably Load Runner, Neo Cloud and Open Source tools, Excels in stakeholder management. You should bring a proven record of delivering complex performance solutions in large\\-scale, distributed environments above all, you should be one living up to IKEA Values.
 



  





**TOGETHER AS A TEAM** 




  





 We set the digital agenda to keep IKEA leading in an uncertain and fast\\-moving environment. We drive the development, provision and operation of our digital products and services through the use of new and existing technology and agile delivery methods to deliver at pace. At the same time, we provide a home for digital skills to both develop and extend the technical, people and business skills needed to ensure continuous development and growth of our digital capability.
